conventions: JUMP REACHABILITY: A character standing on a lower platform and jumping must be able to land on the upper platform. Formula: max_jump_height = jump_speed^2 / (2 * gravity). The TOP EDGE of the upper platform must satisfy: upper_top_y > lower_top_y - max_jump_height + character_height. Example: gravity=0.5, jump_speed=12, max_jump_height=144px, character_height=40px => max platform top-edge difference = 104px.

GROUND TO FIRST PLATFORM: The first reachable platform above the ground MUST be jumpable from ground level. Verify: first_platform_top_y > ground_top_y - (max_jump_height - character_height). This is the most common level design failure.
PLATFORM THICKNESS: Jumpable gap = upper_platform_top_y - lower_platform_top_y. NOT bottom-edge to top-edge. The platform's visual thickness does not add to jumpable distance.
RECOMMENDED PHYSICS: gravity=0.5-0.8 px/frame^2, jump_speed=10-14 px/frame, move_speed=3-5 px/frame. With these params max_jump_height is 90-200px. Platform gaps should be 60-130px.
SCREEN FIT: For fixed-camera games, all level content must fit within canvas width x height. For scrolling games, specify scroll direction explicitly.
PASSAGE WIDTH: Character collision box width < narrowest passage width. All paths must be physically traversable.
MOVING PLATFORM SPEED: Must be <= 80% of character move speed, otherwise character cannot stand stably.
TWO-PLAYER CONTROLS: Two players must use non-conflicting key sets (e.g. WASD + Arrow keys). Never bind the same key to both players.
UNIFORM GRAVITY: All entities subject to gravity must use the same gravity constant. No per-entity gravity unless explicitly designed.
COLLECTIBLE PLACEMENT: Every collectible must be reachable by at least one character via jumping from available platforms.
LIQUID POOL WIDTH: Pool width < max horizontal jump distance. Horizontal jump distance = move_speed * (2 * jump_speed / gravity). Or provide an alternative path (bridge, moving platform).
SELF-CHECK AFTER GENERATION: After generating level data, mentally trace the intended path from ground to exit. Verify every jump is reachable with the physics constants defined.
